claim_text: Patient reports experiencing knee pain for six months.
She is unable to run more than half a mile without pain.
Patient has a history of a broken arm in sixth grade.
Patient denies chest pain.
Patient denies fever.
Patient is taking Ibuprofen.
Patient is taking Aleve.
Patient has a normal gait.
Patient has normal strength in lower extremities bilaterally except abduction.
Abduction strength is 3/5.
Knees show no erythema.
Knees show no ecchymosis.
Knees show no warmth.
Knees show no effusion.
Patient has tenderness to palpation of bilateral retinacular retinaculum.
Patient has a positive patellar grind test.
Patient has symmetrical patellar and Achilles reflexes.
X-Ray findings are normal.
Patient has bilateral knee pain consistent with patellofemoral pain syndrome.
Patient is recommended to undergo physical therapy.
Physical therapy will include exercises to strengthen lower extremities.
Physical therapy will include exercises to increase mobility.
Physical therapy will include exercises to correct running mechanics.
Patient is advised to continue ibuprofen or Aleve as needed for pain relief.
Patient is encouraged to avoid running for now.
A follow-up appointment is scheduled.
